title: TPCH Investment Analyst Output Schema
description: Strict schema for the JSON payload produced by the Investment Analyst agent (both the Supabase run-agent edge function and the local .claude/skills/investment-analyst skill). Any field written into the project_analysis table must conform to this shape. Keep this file aligned with supabase/functions/run-agent/prompt.ts.

project_id.description: UUID or text id of the project in the projects table.

overall_score.description: MUST equal the sum of the five pillar scores.

rating.description: Must match the band implied by overall_score. 80-100 Strong Buy, 60-79 Good Buy, 40-59 Moderate, 0-39 Caution.

headline.description: One or two sentences summarising the project's overall position. No em-dashes, no hype.

summary.description: Overall assessment paragraph. Confident, institutional tone.

warranties.description: Actual warranty details or the literal string 'Data unavailable'. Never null.

memberships.description: Industry memberships, UDIA/MBA/HIA status, or the literal string 'Data unavailable'. Never null.

tpch_assessment.description: The marketer's brief. Names the single strongest selling angle and gives the honest answer to the obvious risk. No em-dashes. No jargon unless described.

sources.description: Flat list of sources referenced anywhere in the narrative. Every inline source citation should appear here.

limitations.description: Optional but strongly encouraged. Honest disclosure of data that could not be verified (e.g. ABS ERP SA2 vintage not surfaced, competitor floor areas not public).

score_reasoning.description: Two or three sentences. Must (a) say why THIS specific number, (b) name the data point or project characteristic pushing it up/down, (c) say what would change it.

stats.description: Exactly three stat keys per pillar, locked to the pillar-specific keys documented in SKILL.md. Validation of key names happens at the consumer level.

narrative.description: Two or three paragraphs. Every factual claim carries an inline (Source: ...) citation.

scarcity_narrative.description: MUST explicitly name at least two currently-selling comparable new developments with individual $/sqm figures (or flag the floor-area limitation honestly where the figure is indicative). An aggregate benchmark alone is insufficient.

x-pre-flight-self-check.description: Non-standard extension. The agent should self-verify these rules before emitting the JSON. Enforcement is at prompt level; validation below the schema should also catch the structural issues.
rules: All five pillars present and keyed: population, economic, supply_demand, affordability, scarcity.
overall_score equals population.score + economic.score + supply_demand.score + affordability.score + scarcity.score.
rating band matches overall_score (80+ Strong Buy, 60-79 Good Buy, 40-59 Moderate, 0-39 Caution).
No em-dash character (U+2014) anywhere in narrative, score_reasoning, headline, summary, or tpch_assessment.
No banned jargon without description: institutional-grade, institutional specification, institutional quality, prime, blue-chip, investment-grade, premium offering, boutique, exclusive.
No XML tags around citations (e.g. <cite>). Citations are inline in plain prose as (Source: ...).
scarcity.scarcity_narrative names at least two specific currently-selling comparable developments with individual $/sqm (or flags floor-area limitation).
affordability.narrative produces per-bedroom like-for-like comparisons (2-bed vs 2-bed, 3-bed vs 3-bed). No mixed-bedroom average vs mixed-suburb median.
trust_governance.warranties and trust_governance.memberships are non-null strings (use 'Data unavailable' if unknown).
Population narrative quotes ABS ERP cat. 3218.0 at SA2 level with vintage explicitly stated, or flags the limitation if the current-vintage figure could not be retrieved.
